pending_articles.title: Monthly Updates [April]
pending_articles.content:
INFO

These update threads only contain major updates. If you're interested in seeing all minor changes you can follow our Commits Page on GitHub or Updates Channel in Discord.

Wiki Updates

  • Added a new Retro Style instance to our backups page. Thank you to ROI for making this. Screenshot / 2

  • Added YouTube Music Tools section to Audio. Thank you to Samidy for the idea.

  • Added Bilibili Tools section to Social Media. Thank you to Waflenoob for the idea.

  • Added Root Managers section to Android. Thank you to SyntaX for the idea.

  • Added Fighting Game section to Gaming Tools.

  • Added Visual Novel section to Gaming Tools.

  • Added LEGO section to Hobbies.

  • Added Karaoke / Synced Lyrics section to Audio.

  • Added Genre Specific Radio section to Audio.

  • Added Flashcard Tools section to Educational.

  • Added History of Technology section to History.

  • Added Declassified / Leaked Documents section to Reading.

  • Updated descriptions + added platform icons to File Converters. Thank you to znarfm for doing this. Before vs After / 2

  • Added platform icons to Note-Taking section. Thank you to RansomQ for doing this. Before vs After / 2

  • Cleaned up Email Aliasing, updated descriptions, and added platform icons. Thank you to goose126 for doing this. Before vs After / 2

  • Cleaned up DNS Filters + moved it out of storage into its own section. Thank you to Waflenoob for doing this. Change List / 2

  • Cleaned up formatting in Audio Ripping Sites. Before vs After / 2

Stars Added ⭐

  • Starred LibreWolf in Browser Privacy. Custom privacy-focused version of Firefox, easily accessible, has good configs, and is well liked.

  • Starred Tuta in Email Privacy. Encrypted email, gives 1GB free, preferred over things like Proton Mail by some.

  • Starred Kettu in Mobile Discord Clients. Modded client for both Android + iOS with plugin support, has been working well for those who have been testing it.

  • Starred Nuvio in Android Streaming. Was already starred in iOS, but Android users seem to be finding it useful. Has plugin support (including stremio), a nice UI, and weekly updates.

  • Starred Qwen + Ask Brave in AI section. Both are unlimited, and their models seem to be working well for people.

  • Starred The OPSEC Bible in Privacy Indexes. In-depth and well respected online anonymity guide.

  • Starred Is it really FOSS in FOSS section. Database that helps determine if something is truly FOSS or not.

  • Starred ScriptCat in Userscript Managers. Open-source, MV3 compatible, allows background + scheduled scripts, considered by some to be the best current script manager.

  • Starred Readest in Ebook Readers. Customizable, supports many formats and platforms, gives free cloud storage w/ progress sync.

  • Starred Flash Museum in Browser Game Sites. Has a clean UI + one of the bigger libraries of oldschool flash games.

  • Starred PressReleased in Paywall Bypass. Based on Bypass Paywalls Clean, requires no extension.

  • Starred Evade in Redirect Bypass. Fast, well maintained, and works on lots of sites.

  • Starred Wiktionary in Dictionary section. Well respected dictionary with lots of active contributors.

  • Starred Mullvad + IVPN in VPN section. Both are confirmed No-Logging / 2, accept multiple payment types, and require no email.

  • Re-starred HolyUnblocker in Proxy Sites. Working well again, has built-in adblock + Tor support.

Things Removed

  • Removed P-Stream as their owners have decided to step back from the project / 2.

  • Removed Tampermonkey as it's had various issues over the years with things like their privacy policy, going closed-source, updates causing data loss, etc. and there's better options anyways (ScriptCat, Violentmonkey, Firemonkey).

  • Removed RTINGS as they've gone fully paid.

  • Unstarred ReVanced as Morphe is now stable enough to replace it + has more active development.

  • Unstarred Trakt as they're now forcing users onto v3 design which is missing many features compared to previous version.

pending_articles.title: Call for Papers: IEEE Transactions on Human-Machine Systems
pending_articles.author: Adriel Carridice

pending_articles.title: Hill-Type Models of Skeletal Muscle and Neuromuscular Actuators: A Systematic Review
pending_articles.content: Backed by a century of research and development, Hill-type models of skeletal muscle, often including a muscle-tendon complex and neuromechanical interface, are widely used for countless applications. Lacking recent comprehensive reviews, the field of Hill-type modeling is, however, dense and hard-to-explore, with detrimental consequences on innovation. Here we present the first systematic review of Hill-type muscle modeling. It aims to clarify the literature by detailing its contents and critically discussing the state-of-the-art by identifying the latest advances, current gaps, and potential future directions in Hill-type modeling. For this purpose, fifty-eight criteria-abiding Hill-type models were assessed according to a completeness evaluation, which identified the modelled muscle properties, and a modeling evaluation, which considered the level of validation and reusability of the models, as well as their modeling strategy and calibration. It is concluded that most models (1) do not significantly advance beyond historical foundational standards, (2) neglect the importance of parameter identification, (3) lack robust validation, and (4) are not reusable in other studies. Besides providing a convenient tool supported by extensive supplementary materials for navigating the literature, the results of this review highlight the need for global recommendations in Hill-type modeling to optimize inter-study consistency, knowledge transfer, and model reusability.

pending_articles.title: fMRI during active GPi DBS uncovers target-specific networks
Prompt-based zero-shot classification for analyzing delusional themes in dreams.
pending_articles.content: This study investigated the use of a large language model, and particularly OpenAI’s Generative Pretrained Transformer (GPT)-4o, for classifying delusional themes in dream reports. Zero-shot classification, where task-specific prompts guided the model without prior fine-tuning, was used to identify three primary categories of delusional themes in 150 culturally diverse dream reports: grandiosity, persecution, and ego-ideal themes. Model outputs were validated against human annotations by a judge who had postgraduate training and was familiar with the categorization of delusional dream themes established by the original author. In addition, the same set of dreams were coded by undergraduate judges to compare the effectiveness of GPT-4o coding. The results showed that GPT-4o achieved an overall accuracy of 97.7%, surpassing the undergraduate annotators’ accuracy of 97.0%. Compared with undergraduate annotators, GPT-4o consistently demonstrated higher agreement with the expert rating (Jaccard index: .589 vs. .199). These findings support the utility of GPT-4o in automated analysis of delusional dream content. (PsycInfo Database Record (c) 2026 APA, all rights reserved)

pending_articles.title: Emotional suppression and the relationship between wakeful and dream emotions.
pending_articles.content: This study aimed to investigate whether emotional suppression tendency is related to the discontinuity between wakeful emotions and dream emotions. Screening and diary surveys were conducted, and a total of 76 participants completed the study. The screening survey measured aspects of dreaming using the Manheim Dream Questionnaire (Schredl et al., 2014), the Emotional Suppression Tendency Scale (Kashimura & Iwamitsu, 2007), and the Japanese version of the Beck Depression Inventory (Hayashi, 1988). During the diary survey, conducted over 7 days, participants completed a presleep task in which they rated the emotions experienced during wakefulness that day and a postsleep task in which they described the content of their dreams and rated the emotions experienced within those dreams. The results confirmed an emotional discontinuity between wakefulness and dreams. Specifically, positive emotions during wakefulness decreased in dreams, whereas negative emotions increased in dreams. This is believed to be due to the emotion-processing function of dreams. However, in the main analyses, no differences in the intensity of emotions were observed according to emotional suppression tendency. This may be related to the fact that the Emotional Suppression Tendency Scale measures the suppression of emotional expression and that the diary method was used as the research approach. (PsycInfo Database Record (c) 2026 APA, all rights reserved)

recently_processed.title: IEEE Brain Joins the American Brain Coalition
recently_processed.content: IEEE Brain is pleased to announce its acceptance as a nonprofit member of the American Brain Coalition (ABC), a prestigious alliance of over 150 organizations dedicated to advancing brain research, advocacy, and improving treatments for individuals affected by brain conditions. The ABC Board has enthusiastically welcomed IEEE Brain into its network, reinforcing a shared commitment to fostering innovation and collaboration ...

recently_processed.title: Cardiovascular and central nervous system risk in astronauts: the minipig as a physiologically relevant radiation model
Changes in resting-state functional connectivity linked to affective symptoms: insights from a population-based study of adolescents and young adults
Self-rated continuity between waking and dreaming: Associations with personality.
recently_processed.content: The continuity hypothesis of dreaming states that our daily activities are reflected in our dreams. The question of whether there are factors, for example, personality, that modulate continuity is still unanswered. The majority of participants of the U.K. library study (N = 1,375) stated that their dreams are affected by their daily activities. This perceived continuity is associated with openness to experience and supports the idea that boundary thinness, which is closely related to openness to experience, should be integrated into the framework of the continuity hypothesis. Moreover, dream recall frequency and female gender were also related to the self-rated continuity between waking and dreaming. This line of research clearly supports the necessity to carry out empirical studies looking at factors that might modulate the continuity between waking and dreaming. (PsycInfo Database Record (c) 2026 APA, all rights reserved)
